title: calandrinia andrewsii
intro: Calandrinia andrewsii, also known as Red Maire or Andrews' Rock Purslane, is a perennial succulent plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Calandrinia andrewsii include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote wound healing, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Calandrinia andrewsii typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves or roots, which can be used to create teas, salves, or poultices.
